content: The language learning sense of reflexion as deliberate metacognitive review of one’s own output can be adapted to improve LLM self-reflection mechanisms, by framing LLM self-reflection as a structured vocabulary and output revision exercise analogous to how high school students refine their writing in a reading and writing workshop. This framing naturally creates a graded, incremental self-reflection routine instead of the inconsistent single-step multi-perspective reflection most current LLM systems rely on.
